info.title: Auth API
info.description: Authentication: credentials, JWT access tokens and rotating opaque refresh tokens.

post.description: Authenticates a user and returns a JWT access token with an opaque refresh token.

200.description: Authenticated; a new session is opened.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Request to authenticate with existing credentials.

post.description: Revokes the presented opaque refresh token.

200.description: Refresh token revoked; the session is ended.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Request to revoke a refresh token and end its session.

post.description: Rotates an opaque refresh token and returns a new JWT access token with a new refresh token.

200.description: Tokens rotated; the previous refresh token is revoked.

401.description: Refresh token is unknown, expired, or was already rotated (possible theft - the whole session is revoked).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Request to exchange a refresh token for a new token pair.

post.description: Creates credentials and returns a JWT access token with an opaque refresh token.

201.description: Account created; a new session is opened.

409.description: An account with this email already exists.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

code.description:
The status code, which should be an enum value of
[google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description:
A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any
user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the
[google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized
by the client.

details.description:
A list of messages that carry the error details.  There is a common set of
message types for APIs to use.

rpcStatus.description:
The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for
different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is
used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains
three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details.

You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the
[API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

v1LoginRequest.description: Request to authenticate with existing credentials.

tokens.description: Token pair for the created session.

v1LogoutRequest.description: Request to revoke a refresh token and end its session.

refreshToken.description: Current opaque refresh token. It is revoked and replaced on success.

v1RefreshRequest.description: Request to exchange a refresh token for a new token pair.

tokens.description: New token pair. The previously presented refresh token is no longer valid.

email.description: Email used as the login identifier. Must be unique.

password.description:
Plain-text password. Limited to 72 bytes because bcrypt silently
truncates longer input.

tokens.description: Token pair for the newly created session.

accessToken.description:
Short-lived JWT used to authorize API requests via the
"Authorization: Bearer {token}" header.

accessExpiresAt.description: Moment when the access token expires and must be refreshed.

refreshToken.description:
Long-lived opaque token used only to obtain a new pair via Refresh.
Rotated on every use and stored hashed server-side.

refreshExpiresAt.description: Moment when the refresh token expires; after that a full login is required.

v1TokenPair.description: A pair of tokens issued after successful authentication.
